headline: The Subsidy That Costs More Than the Subsidy
context: Government proposed targeted fuel subsidies. Projected savings: RM2.1B. The system to deliver those savings costs RM1.8B.

cards.big: Save RM2.1 billion. Spend RM1.8 billion doing it.
cards.sub: Net saving: RM300M. That's 14%, not the 40% in headlines.

cards.big: Six agencies must share data in real time. None can.
cards.sub: The government's data integration project: 23% complete after 3 years.

cards.big: Targeted subsidies exclude 15–25% of eligible people.
cards.sub: 1.2 million Malaysian adults lack income documentation.

cards.big: Framed as responsibility vs populism. Actually it's math.
cards.sub: Does admin cost + exclusion cost exceed leakage cost?

cards.big: What if deciding who deserves help costs more than helping everyone?
The numbers make targeting weaker than the headlines suggest.
